text: This is the VOA Special English Technology Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语科技报道。

text: Benjamin Stassen took his own life in 2010.
zh: 本杰明·斯塔森于2010年自杀身亡。

text: His parents thought their 21 -year -old son was happy.
zh: 他的父母原以为这个21岁的儿子过得很快乐。

text: Since his suicide, they have attempted to learn why he killed himself.
zh: 自他自杀以来，他们一直试图弄清楚他为什么结束自己的生命。

text: Alice and Jay Stassen thought Benjamin's Facebook account might hold clues to his suicide.
zh: 爱丽丝和杰伊·斯塔森认为本杰明的Facebook账户或许能提供一些线索。

text: But the account does not belong to them, nor did it belong to their son.
zh: 但这个账户并不属于他们，也不属于他们的儿子。

text: Facebook owns everything in his account.
zh: Facebook拥有账户里的一切内容。

text: That is what the company says in its user agreement.
zh: 这是该公司在用户协议中所声明的。

text: J. Stassen says he found it difficult even to communicate with Facebook.
zh: 杰伊·斯塔森说，他甚至很难与Facebook取得联系。

text: If you search on the homepage of Facebook for an email address, a mailing address, a phone number,
zh: 如果你在Facebook主页上搜索电子邮件地址、邮寄地址、电话号码，

text: or a contact person to help, he says, you will find little information.
zh: 或者想找一个能提供帮助的联系人，他说，你几乎找不到什么信息。

text: A court ordered the company to let the Stassens
zh: 一家法院已下令该公司允许斯塔森夫妇

text: see their son's account, but Facebook has yet to obey the order.
zh: 查看他们儿子的账户，但Facebook至今仍未遵守这一命令。

text: 34 -year -old Mac Tonys died in his sleep in
zh: 34岁的马克·托尼斯在睡梦中去世，那是在

text: 2009. He left behind a lot of online friends,
zh: 2009年。他留下了许多网上好友，

text: many of whom liked his futuristic blog, Post Human Blues.
zh: 其中许多人喜欢他那个充满未来色彩的博客《后人类忧郁》。

text: Work on the blog stopped when he died. A short time later,
zh: 他去世后，博客的更新就停止了。不久之后，

text: the comments area was filled with unwanted advertising. That angered his friend,
zh: 评论区被大量垃圾广告淹没。这让他的朋友非常愤怒，

text: Dia Soban. He said the advertising, or spam, dishonored his friend.
zh: 迪亚·索班说，这些垃圾广告是对朋友的不敬。

text: Lawyer John Boucher stays informed about digital rights and
zh: 律师约翰·布歇一直关注数字权利和

text: the law. But he admits that he and his wife
zh: 相关法律问题。但他承认，他和妻子

text: have signed many user agreements without reading them.
zh: 也曾在没有阅读的情况下签署了许多用户协议。

text: He says he would not know how to get information from his wife's accounts if she dies.
zh: 他说，如果妻子去世，他甚至不知道该如何获取她账户中的信息。

text: Mr. Boucher says he thinks that laws will be written
zh: 布歇先生认为，随着时间推移，

text: over time to deal with these situations.
zh: 相关法律终会被制定出来以应对这些情况，

text: but it will take a long time to be accepted by the states.
zh: 但要被各州普遍接受还需要很长时间。

text: Some businesses now are offering people a way to control what happens to their online information
zh: 目前一些企业开始提供服务，让人们能够掌控自己去世后网络信息的处理方式。

text: after they die. One way to do this
zh: 其中一种方法是，

text: is to place your online accounts and passwords in a digital storage area.
zh: 把你的网络账户和密码存放在一个数字存储空间中。

text: You give the owners of the area orders about which information to destroy
zh: 你可以事先指示该平台的管理者，哪些信息应被销毁，

text: and which to give others when you die.
zh: 哪些信息在你去世后可以交给他人。

text: Mark Platner was one of Mac Tony's friends.
zh: 马克·普拉特纳是马克·托尼的朋友之一。

text: He recently used a program called SightSucker to download Mac's blog.
zh: 他最近用一款名为SightSucker的程序下载了马克的博客内容。

text: He then uploaded a copy of the blog to a new website under his control.
zh: 然后把博客内容上传到了一个由他自己管理的新网站上。

text: Mark Plattner says we should all plan our own digital legacy.
zh: 马克·普拉特纳说，我们都应该规划好自己的数字遗产。

text: He says don't be passive. Get to work on your online afterlife now.
zh: 他说，不要被动等待，现在就该着手安排自己的网络身后事。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Technology Report.
zh: 以上就是美国之音特别英语科技报道。

text: You You can leave comments about this story
zh: 你可以在voaspecialenglish.com

text: at voaspecialenglish .com. I'm Carolyn Presutti.
zh: 网站上留言分享你的看法。我是卡罗琳·普雷苏蒂。
